After studying this lesson, you will be able to:

payload.file_name: LOMA281_M1L2_Knowledge File_Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies
payload.summary: LOMA 281 Module 1 Lesson 2 Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies Summary of Key Points The financial services industry includes financial institutions that help people save, borrow, invest, and manage money. (Examples: banks, insurance companies, brokerage firms, mutual fund companies, and finance companies) Deregulation and other factors have contributed to the removal of barriers that historically prevented different financial institutions from competing with each other. This evolution of the insurance industry is characterized by • Convergence • Consolidation • Globalization Insurance companies sell products that help people manage • Personal risk • Property damage risk • Liability risk All life insurance companies are corporations that are distinct from their owners. Typically, insurance companies are organized as stock insurance companies, mutual insurance companies, or fraternal benefit societies. The primary goals of insurance regulation are to make sure insurers • Remain solvent—able to pay all obligations on time • Conduct their business fairly, honestly, and competently In the United States, insurance is regulated mainly by state insurance departments. States oversee insurers’ • Market conduct practices • Financial condition • Policy forms The federal government regulates • Sales of variable life insurance and annuities • Consumer protections • Consumer privacy • Income taxes • Antiterrorism Federal laws pertaining to insurance industry: McCarran-Ferguson Act and Dodd-Frank Act, which established the Federal Insurance Office (FIO). regulation (quy định bảo hiểm) in most countries.

governments (chính quyền liên bang) with respect (quản lý) to
insurance regulation (quy định bảo hiểm).

Within these three sectors operate many different types of financial
institutions.

Globalization
Another trend impacting financial institutions is globalization.
Financial services institutions, particularly those from Western Europe and
North America, have been expanding their customer bases in nations outside
their countries of origin.
Often companies have to purchase financial institutions in other countries to
gain entry into foreign markets.

insurance department.

Explanation:
Variable life insurance and variable annuities are considered to be
investment products and so are regulated by the SEC. The owner, not the
insurer, assumes most, if not all, of the investment risk for a variable
product. Individuals selling variable insurance products must obtain both a
state insurance license and a FINRA license to sell investment securities.

Course: LOMA 281 - Meeting Customer Needs with Insurance and Annuities - 3rd Edition | Module: Module 1 - Risk and Insurance | Lesson: Lesson 2 - Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies | Source name: LOMA281_M1L2_Knowledge File_Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies | Definition: State guaranty associations protect policyholders if an insurer becomes insolvent, covering benefits typically up to $300,000. | Domain Tags: Guaranty Associations, Insolvency Protection, Insurance | Related Nodes: KN010 | Category: Consumer Protection | Node Name: State Guaranty Associations | Chunk Text Content: Other Federal Laws
Even federal laws that aren’t designed to regulate insurance directly affect
the insurance industry. Here are some examples.
Consumer Protection Laws Consumer protection laws prohibit insurers from
engaging in unfair competition or unfair or deceptive business practices,
primarily related to interstate advertising.
Privacy Laws Privacy laws affect how insurers collect and use personal
information about their customers.

payload.file_name: LOMA281_M1L2_Knowledge File_Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies
payload.summary: LOMA 281 Module 1 Lesson 2 Organization and Regulation of Insurance Companies Summary of Key Points The financial services industry includes financial institutions that help people save, borrow, invest, and manage money. (Examples: banks, insurance companies, brokerage firms, mutual fund companies, and finance companies) Deregulation and other factors have contributed to the removal of barriers that historically prevented different financial institutions from competing with each other. This evolution of the insurance industry is characterized by • Convergence • Consolidation • Globalization Insurance companies sell products that help people manage • Personal risk • Property damage risk • Liability risk All life insurance companies are corporations that are distinct from their owners. Typically, insurance companies are organized as stock insurance companies, mutual insurance companies, or fraternal benefit societies. The primary goals of insurance regulation are to make sure insurers • Remain solvent—able to pay all obligations on time • Conduct their business fairly, honestly, and competently In the United States, insurance is regulated mainly by state insurance departments. States oversee insurers’ • Market conduct practices • Financial condition • Policy forms The federal government regulates • Sales of variable life insurance and annuities • Consumer protections • Consumer privacy • Income taxes • Antiterrorism Federal laws pertaining to insurance industry: McCarran-Ferguson Act and Dodd-Frank Act, which established the Federal Insurance Office (FIO). In each state in the United States, an organization that protects
policyowners, insureds, beneficiaries, annuitants (người nhận niên kim),
payees, and assignees (người được chuyển nhượng) against losses that might
result from the impairment (sựsuy giảm năng lực tài chính) or insolvency
(mất thanh toán) of a life or health insurer that does business in the state.
Insurers are required to participate in a state’s guaranty association in
order to be licensed in that state.
